She is highly regarded for her ability to simplify complex financial matters, helping clients feel at ease during tax lodgements. Customers appreciate her prompt and reliable communication, as well as her willingness to assist with urgent or last-minute requests.

Nexo Accounting provides a comprehensive range of services. These include individual tax returns, Business Activity Statements (BAS), partnership and company formation, trust setups, and Self-Managed Superannuation Fund (SMSF) tax returns. They also act as an ASIC agent, supporting businesses with registration and corporate compliance. With a client-focused approach and transparent pricing, the firm offers dependable support tailored to the needs of local residents and business owners.

Clients highly value the practice for its professional, efficient, and friendly approach. The firm has also been recognised for assisting clients with company registration, managing student visa tax returns, and providing proactive advice to help clients stay compliant.

Key services offered include individual and business income tax returns, BAS and GST lodgements, bookkeeping, payroll services, financial reporting, and tax planning. Operating from Willard Road, PNP Tax & Accounting offers the flexibility of in-person appointments as well as remote phone and video consultations, making expert financial guidance accessible to the local Truganina community and clients nationwide.

candidates.description:
ALiN Cargo Express Melbourne Pty Ltd is a dedicated freight forwarding service specialising in door-to-door cargo delivery from Victoria to the Philippines. Located in Maidstone, this local branch helps the Filipino-Australian community stay connected with family back home by providing reliable logistics solutions.

They offer both sea cargo and air cargo shipping options for sending balikbayan boxes, personal items, and commercial goods. For sea freight, they run regular shipment schedules, while air cargo shipments have convenient bi-weekly cut-off dates. To make sending gifts and packages even easier, they also provide parcel receiving, secure storage, and box consolidation services. This allows customers to shop from multiple online stores and combine their purchases into a single shipment to save on shipping costs.

The Melbourne branch operates from Monday to Saturday, between 8:00 am and 5:00 pm, and is closed on Sundays. Their friendly local team is always ready to assist with booking pick-ups, checking rates, or preparing packaging materials. Whether you are sending a single balikbayan box or organising a large commercial shipment, they ensure your packages reach their destination safely and efficiently.
candidates.name: ALIN CARGO EXPRESS MELBOURNE PTY LTD
EMG Cargo Express
candidates.description:
EMG Cargo Express is a family-owned freight forwarding service based in Hampton Park, Melbourne. Managed by Ed and Obet Guevarra, the business specialises in sending door-to-door Balikbayan box shipments from Victoria to the Philippines. They are an officially accredited sea freight forwarder by the Philippine Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) Fair Trade Enforcement Bureau. This ensures all cargo is handled in accordance with official safety and customs standards.

The service specialises in transporting personal effects, gifts, and essential items safely to family and friends back home. Customers appreciate the reliability and security offered when sending their parcels across the sea. They offer competitive rates depending on the destination region in the Philippines, including Metro Manila, Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao.

With years of experience in cargo handling, the team provides a personalised service to the local Filipino-Australian community. They also support local community events and gatherings across Melbourne, fostering strong cultural ties. Whether you are sending a standard Balikbayan box or organising a larger shipment, EMG Cargo Express helps connect families across borders.

International Cargo Express Pty Ltd. is a reputable and highly experienced freight forwarding and customs brokerage service situated in Tullamarine, Melbourne. Established in 1988, this dedicated logistics provider specialise in managing international sea and air shipments from Europe, the USA, and Asia.

Local clients highly recommend the company for its exceptionally structured and customer-focused approach. Customers appreciate having dedicated account managers who provide consistent daily updates, ensuring complete transparency throughout the shipping process. The professional and proactive team, including Roberto Rivas, Annie, Michael, Rob, and Marcus, are frequently praised for their responsiveness, friendly support, and helpfulness in explaining complex logistical processes. Additionally, the in-house customs department is widely noted for its expertise in customs clearance and managing tariff concessions.

Whether handling personal cargo or commercial business shipments, they deliver dependable end-to-end logistics solutions. Conveniently located near Melbourne Airport, the Tullamarine office is open from Monday to Friday, between 8:30 am and 5:00 pm, providing a reliable and personalised alternative to larger cargo providers for businesses across Victoria.
candidates.name: International Cargo Express Pty Ltd.
Sari Sari Asian Groceries
candidates.description:
Sari Sari Asian Groceries is a welcoming Filipino grocery store located in Narre Warren Village, Melbourne. It serves as a reliable local hub for the Filipino-Australian community, offering an extensive selection of essential ingredients and pantry staples. Customers appreciate the wide range of chips, Datu Puti sauces, Mama Sita's mixes, and baking syrups available on the shelves. A key highlight for locals is the freshly prepared, highly popular Filipino pork barbecue served every Saturday.

The store is well-regarded for its warm and friendly customer service, with staff members like Gina and Cherry often praised for their helpfulness and hospitality. Beyond groceries, the business provides a convenient money transfer service to the Philippines, helping community members send support back home. Additionally, the shop focuses on eco-friendly practices by offering cardboard boxes for packaging instead of plastic bags. Situated on Webb Street, it is a favourite spot to find familiar tastes from home in a clean and supportive environment.

Sari-Sari Asian Groceries is a beloved Filipino and Asian grocery store nestled in Narre Warren Village, Melbourne. The shop serves as a convenient hub for the local Filipino-Australian community, offering not only essential pantry staples but also money remittance services directly to the Philippines.

Customers frequently praise the warm, hospitable service provided by the friendly staff, including local favourites Cherry and Ate Gina. Inside the clean and tidy store, visitors can find a comprehensive range of authentic products. Key items include a variety of crunchy chips, baking syrups, Mama Sita’s mixes, and Datu Puti sauces. A highlight of the weekly calendar is the freshly prepared hot pork barbecue served every Saturday, which has become a must-try local tradition.

Additionally, the business promotes eco-friendly practices by offering reusable boxes instead of single-use plastic bags for purchases. Located on Webb Street, it is a reliable and welcoming spot for anyone looking to stock up on their favourite Filipino treats or send support to family back home.

The Association of Filipino-Australian Golden Age of Victoria (AFAGAVI) is a registered community organisation based in Sunshine, Victoria. Established in 2002, this dedicated association has spent over two decades fostering camaraderie, mutual support, and social connection among Filipino senior citizens across the state. As an active affiliate of the Filipino Community Council of Victoria (FCCVI), the group plays a vital role in keeping older Filipino-Australians connected to their rich cultural heritage and to one another.

AFAGAVI organises a range of recreational activities, social gatherings, and community events designed specifically for seniors. These gatherings help members stay active, share stories, and build meaningful friendships, creating a supportive network that reduces isolation. By cooperating closely with peak multicultural bodies, the organisation also helps members access essential aged care resources and advocacy services. Located in the multicultural hub of Sunshine, the association welcomes seniors who wish to participate in cultural preservation and community connection in a warm and welcoming environment.
candidates.name: Association of Filipino-Australian Golden Age of Victoria
Filipino-Australian Lawyers Association of Victoria (FALAW)
candidates.description:
The Filipino-Australian Lawyers Association of Victoria (FALAW) is a vibrant professional organisation and community association dedicated to empowering Filipino-Australian legal professionals, barristers, and law students. With a mission centred on celebrating cultural heritage and advocating for equity, diversity, and meaningful representation within the Australian legal landscape, FALAW provides an essential network for networking, mentorship, and professional development.

The association is actively involved in community support and legal education, organising programmes like the Law Unlocked Series, which delivers practical legal awareness on topics such as tenancy rights and dispute resolution. FALAW also hosts the FALAW Forward podcast to amplify Filipino voices and discuss key industry issues, including representation and automated decision-making. To assist the broader community, the association recently launched the FALAW Directory, connecting individuals with specialised legal professionals across Victoria and Canberra. Through collaborations with local bodies like the Philippine Consulate General in Melbourne, FALAW participates in vital forums addressing family law and access to justice. Whether you are a legal practitioner looking to expand your network or a student seeking guidance, FALAW offers a collaborative space to grow and make a positive impact. Inquiries can be sent directly to falaw@falaw.org.

candidates.name: Australian-Filipino Community Services
candidates.description:
Australian-Filipino Community Services (AFCS) is a dedicated not-for-profit organisation based in Doveton, Victoria. Operating from Doveton Baptist Church and Maharlika Lodge, the group supports the health, social, and welfare needs of the Filipino-Australian community. Local community members and volunteers describe the organisation as a warm, welcoming environment that truly feels like a home away from home.

AFCS specialises in delivering essential services, including aged care programs, dementia-specific support, and volunteer-led community assistance. To promote wellbeing and cultural pride, the group runs a diverse range of activities. These include 'SARAP' home-cooking workshops for youth under 25, community sports teams like the 'Kalabaws' footy squad, and cultural dance academies teaching traditional dances like Tinikling. They also host family-friendly creative sessions like sketch and sip, alongside regional events such as Christmas in July celebrations in Gippsland and annual flag-raising ceremonies for Philippine Independence Day.

Whether you are looking for support services, wish to participate in youth leadership programs, or want to connect with cultural events, AFCS provides a supportive hub for the local diaspora. You can reach their team at their Doveton centre, via email at info@afcs.org.au, or by calling (03) 9701 3421.

candidates.name: Balikbayan Groceries
candidates.description:
Balikbayan Groceries is a clean and well-organised Filipino convenience store located in Kensington, just a short distance from the Melbourne CBD. Easily accessible via the tram on Racecourse Road or by car with plenty of parking on Stubbs Street, this local shop specialises in authentic goods from the Philippines and Latin America.

Inside, visitors will find a wide variety of pantry staples, unique snacks, and frozen favourites such as longganisa, sisig, and tender juicy hotdogs. The shop is also a popular spot for ube lovers, stocking everything from ube ice cream and cakes to frozen paste and chips. Alongside Filipino essentials, customers can explore a diverse range of Mexican and Colombian products, including arepas, pandebono, and Colombian coffee.

Run by a friendly team, including Tony, May, and Chris, the store is highly regarded for its warm and welcoming customer service. The staff are always happy to help, even assisting shoppers step-by-step with online orders over the phone. Balikbayan Groceries also offers convenient home delivery options, a local loyalty rewards program, and Ria Money Transfer services, making it a valuable hub for the local community.

Balikbayan Groceries Truganina was a local specialty grocery store located in Truganina, a suburb in Melbourne's outer west. The business specialised in importing and retailing a comprehensive range of traditional food products, pantry staples, and native delicacies from the Philippines, bringing the authentic taste of home to the local Filipino-Australian community.

The shop offered a wide selection of everyday essentials, including popular condiments like cane vinegar, soy sauce, and banana ketchup, alongside sweet treats, frozen items, and ingredients required for cooking classic dishes at home. It provided a welcoming space for residents in the western suburbs to find specialty brand-name products not commonly stocked in major supermarkets.

This physical warehouse location in Truganina has permanently closed, and the business now operates out of its main retail storefront located in Kensington, Victoria.

candidates.description:
Barkada is a casual Filipino eatery located at 70 Speakmen Street in Kensington, Melbourne. The restaurant brings authentic Filipino street food flavours to the inner west, offering dine-in, takeaway, and delivery options.

Diners can enjoy a variety of traditional grilled dishes, known as inihaw. Popular menu choices include marinated pork barbecue skewers, chargrilled chicken inasal, and crispy pork belly liempo. For those looking for classic street food delicacies, Barkada serves skewers of pork ears, chicken feet, and pork intestines. Classic rice combo meals and sizzlers, such as sisig, are also available. To finish the meal, the kitchen prepares sweet Filipino desserts like halo-halo and mais con yelo.

The eatery opens daily from 10:00 am to 7:00 pm, providing convenient access for lunch and early dinner. With friendly service and a relaxed atmosphere, this local dining spot is ideal for sharing a meal with friends and family. It offers a taste of traditional Manila-style street dining in the heart of Melbourne.

candidates.name: Barkada Pinoy
candidates.description:
Barkada Pinoy is a cosy, hidden Filipino dining spot and grocery store located in the heart of Melbourne's CBD. Tucked away in a laneway on Sutherland Street and situated up a set of stairs, this venue has relocated from its former Melbourne Central home to offer a warm, welcoming vibe reminiscent of a traditional Filipino neighbourhood shop (tindahan).

The menu specialises in authentic street food and classic grilled offerings. Regulars highly recommend starting with the chicken inasal, which is freshly marinated and grilled to order, alongside their signature pork sisig combo. The restaurant also serves a unique array of street food skewers, including pork intestine (isaw), crispy chicken skin, and battered quail eggs (kwek kwek). For those looking to satisfy a sweet craving, the taho and refreshing, lightly sweetened halo-halo are popular choices. A small pantry section stocked with popular Filipino goods completes the experience.

With helpful staff, convenient QR-code ordering, and a charming facade displaying nostalgic childhood candies and staples, Barkada Pinoy provides a genuine slice of Manila in the city centre. It is an ideal destination for anyone homesick or curious to explore traditional Filipino flavours.

candidates.description:
Barkada Pinoy Streetfood was a popular Filipino street food restaurant located in the Melbourne Central Shopping Centre. The eatery specialised in charcoal-grilled skewered meats and traditional street food delicacies, bringing a taste of Filipino dining culture to the Melbourne CBD.

Customers loved the casual atmosphere and watching the chefs grill skewered meats over real coal through the window. The menu featured pork and chicken skewers basted in a sweet and savoury marinade, crispy chicken skin, and grilled pork intestines. Guests also enjoyed classic street food items such as kwek kwek, which are deep-fried battered quail eggs served with sweet or vinegar dipping sauces. For dessert, the restaurant offered traditional favourites, including halo-halo, taho, and saba con yelo.

The friendly service and central location made it a popular spot for shoppers and city workers looking for a quick lunch or dinner. This business has permanently closed.

Barkada Pinoy Street Food was a casual Filipino eatery located at the Highpoint Shopping Centre in Maribyrnong, Victoria. Specialising in authentic Pinoy street food and grilled delicacies, this food stall was a popular spot for shoppers seeking a quick and flavourful meal. The establishment brought the vibrant flavours of Manila's street food culture to Melbourne's western suburbs, serving as a favourite destination for both the local Filipino community and curious food lovers.

The menu featured traditional charcoal-grilled skewers, including highly popular pork and chicken options glazed in a sweet and savoury barbecue sauce. Visitors frequently praised the Highpoint branch for serving particularly tender and well-seasoned meat compared to other locations. In addition to classic skewers, the stall offered deep-fried street snacks, ube hopia, and silken taho. Diners also enjoyed a comforting side of warm bone broth with their meals.

Operating from a casual outdoor setup, the stall provided a small dining area with tables and chairs for customers to enjoy their food straight off the grill. The staff members were widely appreciated for their friendly service and ability to handle busy periods with a smile. This business has permanently closed.

Cebuano Association of Australia, Inc.
candidates.description:
The Cebuano Association of Australia, Inc. (CAA) is a non-profit community organisation established in 2012 in Victoria. The association aims to support Filipinos of Cebuano descent and ancestry while fostering a warm, inclusive community spirit across Melbourne.

Members value the organisation's outstanding dedication to preserving and promoting Cebuano culture and heritage. The association is well known for organising vibrant cultural events, including the annual Sinulog Festival celebrations featuring traditional Catholic masses and colourful processions to honour the Santo Niño. In addition to cultural festivities, the group coordinates friendly sports events such as basketball tournaments, social gatherings, and fundraising gala nights that bring families together.

A major focus of the association is conducting humanitarian aid and disaster relief work. The group regularly organises successful fundraising campaigns and donation drives to assist communities in Cebu during natural disasters like typhoons and earthquakes. They also provide welcome support, networking, and practical guidance for newly arrived Cebuanos settling into their new lives in Australia.

This association has permanently closed.

candidates.name: Ilonggo Association of Victoria, Australia Inc
candidates.description:
The Ilonggo Association of Victoria, Australia Inc (IAVIA) is a registered non-profit community organisation based in Melbourne, Victoria, established in 1986. Formed by Ilonggo migrants from the Western Visayas region of the Philippines, the association is dedicated to supporting its members, promoting cultural customs and Hiligaynon/Ilonggo traditions, and assisting with local assimilation in Australia.

A key focus of the association is its humanitarian and educational initiatives. The organisation actively supports charitable projects in the Western Visayas, including providing essential scholarship grants to deserving students at the Central Philippine University. Through these efforts, the association helps create educational opportunities for youth in their home provinces.

Within Victoria, the association is a prominent and active member of the Filipino-Australian community. It participates in cultural events such as the Philippine Independence Day commemorations and collaborates closely with peak bodies like the Filipino Community Council of Victoria (FCCVI) to support community welfare. Through social gatherings, dinner dances, and fundraising events, the association continues to foster camaraderie and cultural pride among Ilonggo-Australians in Victoria.

Christ's Commission Fellowship (CCF) Melbourne is a welcoming, family-oriented Filipino-led Christian church that serves as a spiritual home for the Filipino-Australian community. As a unique feature, FAFAG is the only Filipino club in Geelong that owns its own dedicated building, known as the D.W. Hope Centre in Norlane.

candidates.description:
Inasal Express is a popular Filipino restaurant located in the Ella Precinct of Melbourne Central Shopping Centre. This eatery specialises in authentic, casual Filipino street food and comforting classic dishes. It is a convenient go-to spot for locals and visitors craving a quick, satisfying meal in a welcoming setting.

Customers frequently praise the signature Chicken Inasal, particularly the juicy and tender thigh portion, which is perfectly grilled to deliver a rich, smoky flavour. Other savoury favourites include the flavourful pork barbecue skewers, bangus sisig, and grilled liempo. Diners can also upgrade their meals with aromatic java rice or garlic rice. To finish the dining experience, the menu features iconic Filipino desserts and refreshing drinks, such as creamy halo-halo, ice scramble, buko pandan, and nostalgic sago gulaman.

While most reviews highlight the friendly, attentive customer service and the vibrant atmosphere that showcases Philippine culture, some diners note occasional consistency issues with food seasoning. Overall, the warm hospitality and generous portions make Inasal Express a popular destination for anyone wanting to explore traditional Filipino flavours in the heart of Melbourne.

candidates.name: Inasal Express - Grazeland
candidates.description:
Inasal Express is a vibrant Filipino eatery located in the popular Grazeland food marketplace in Spotswood, Melbourne. Specialising in authentic grilled delicacies, this stall brings the rich, smoky flavours of traditional Filipino street food to local food lovers. The star of the menu is their signature Chicken Inasal, a classic marinated grilled chicken dish that is celebrated for its savoury profile and tender texture. 

Beyond their famous chicken, the menu features a variety of beloved Filipino dishes, including crispy pata, lechon kawali, and flavourful grilled pork ribs. Seafood lovers can enjoy grilled milkfish and salmon. Guests can pair these mains with a choice of aromatic java rice, bagoong rice, or garlic rice to complete the traditional dining experience. For a sweet finish, the refreshing mango sago dessert is a highly recommended favourite.

Locals and visitors praise the friendly customer service and welcoming atmosphere at Grazeland. Operating from Friday evening through Sunday, Inasal Express is a perfect spot for families and friends to gather and enjoy a hearty meal. Whether grabbing a quick takeaway or sitting down to dine in the lively outdoor market, this eatery provides a wonderful introduction to Filipino cuisine in Melbourne's inner west.

Locals highly recommend their signature charcoal-grilled pork skewers, chicken BBQ, and isaw (grilled pork small intestines) paired with their special vinegar dip. candidates.description:

The restaurant is famous for its signature seafood boils, served in the traditional Kamayan or Boodle Fight style where guests enjoy meals directly from the table. Customers highly recommend the rich, coconutty Kalye sauce, which perfectly pairs with prawns, mussels, clams, and crab. Beyond seafood, the menu features classic Pinoy favourites such as sizzling pork sisig, crispy pata, and skewers like pork intestine (isaw). For lunch, popular silog dishes like tapsilog and bangsilog are served with garlic rice and fried eggs.

To finish the meal, diners rave about the creative dessert selection, particularly the halo-halo snow and buko pandan bingsu topped with thick ube ice cream. The bright, beautifully decorated dining space is fully licensed and allows BYO wine, making it a favourite spot for family gatherings and celebrations. candidates.description:

It specialises in bringing authentic Filipino flavours and a unique communal dining experience to the local community. The venue is highly popular for its signature seafood boils, which features fresh prawns, mussels, and mud crab tossed in rich sauces such as garlic butter or the spicy shabang sauce. Diners can enjoy this meal in the traditional Kamayan or boodle fight style, where food is shared and eaten with hands directly from banana leaves.
